items.prompt: Compute `3/4 + 1/8` by rewriting to common units and show the equivalent fractions you used.
items.expected_evidence: place X marks correctly above measurement values on a line plot
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates place X marks correctly above measurement values on a line plot with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Compute `5/6 - 1/4` and justify the common denominator choice.
items.expected_evidence: interpret repeated measures and identify most/least frequent values
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates interpret repeated measures and identify most/least frequent values with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Place `2/3` and `5/8` on the same number line and determine which is greater.
items.expected_evidence: use the line plot to answer additive and comparison questions
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates use the line plot to answer additive and comparison questions with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why `3/4` can be rewritten as `6/8` without changing its value.
items.expected_evidence: place X marks correctly above measurement values on a line plot
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to place X marks correctly above measurement values on a line plot.

items.prompt: Explain why fractions can only be combined directly when they refer to equal-sized parts.
items.expected_evidence: interpret repeated measures and identify most/least frequent values
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to interpret repeated measures and identify most/least frequent values.

items.prompt: A student says `3/4 + 1/8 = 4/12`. Diagnose the first structural mistake. Focus on mark position error.
items.expected_evidence: place X marks correctly above measurement values on a line plot
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward place X marks correctly above measurement values on a line plot.

items.prompt: A student rewrites `2/3` as `3/4` before adding. Identify the equivalence error. Focus on frequency tracking loss.
items.expected_evidence: interpret repeated measures and identify most/least frequent values
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward interpret repeated measures and identify most/least frequent values.

items.prompt: A recipe uses `3/4` cup of oats and `1/8` cup of seeds. How much is used altogether?
items.expected_evidence: place X marks correctly above measurement values on a line plot
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves place X marks correctly above measurement values on a line plot in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: A hiker walks `5/6` mile in the morning and `1/4` mile less in the afternoon. How much farther was the morning walk?
items.expected_evidence: interpret repeated measures and identify most/least frequent values
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves interpret repeated measures and identify most/least frequent values in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: After a delay, solve `2/3 + 1/6` and explain why the rewrite keeps the quantity unchanged.
items.expected_evidence: place X marks correctly above measurement values on a line plot
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates place X marks correctly above measurement values on a line plot on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Before closing line plots and measurement data, complete a prerequisite check on scaled bar graphs and numerical data and explain how that prior understanding supports the fraction reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether scaled bar graphs and numerical data is secure enough to support line plots and measurement data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in scaled bar graphs and numerical data from a current-node issue in line plots and measurement data.
items.suspected_knowledge_gap_if_incorrect: scaled bar graphs and numerical data

items.prompt: Use a quick standard length units and tool use probe to determine whether the error belongs to the prerequisite or to line plots and measurement data.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether standard length units and tool use is secure enough to support line plots and measurement data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in standard length units and tool use from a current-node issue in line plots and measurement data.
items.suspected_knowledge_gap_if_incorrect: standard length units and tool use
